# Navigation Menu

Organizes important site destinations into links and expandable navigation panels.

## Installation

Add the Navigation Menu component from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/navigation-menu
```

## Usage

Use Navigation Menu for a small set of site-level destinations where some categories benefit from a preview panel. Keep direct destinations as links and use triggers only for items that open content. Prefer plain links for a simple header and Sidebar for persistent application navigation with many destinations.

**Navigation menu example**

```vue
<template>
  <NavigationMenu>
    <NavigationMenuList>
      <NavigationMenuItem>
        <NavigationMenuTrigger>Getting started</NavigationMenuTrigger>
        <NavigationMenuContent>
          <ul class="grid w-96 gap-2 p-4">
            <li>
              <NavigationMenuLink href="/docs">
                <span class="font-medium">Introduction</span>
                <span class="text-muted-foreground">Learn how StackTrace UI is structured.</span>
              </NavigationMenuLink>
            </li>
            <li>
              <NavigationMenuLink href="/docs/installation">
                <span class="font-medium">Installation</span>
                <span class="text-muted-foreground">Configure the registry and add components.</span>
              </NavigationMenuLink>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </NavigationMenuContent>
      </NavigationMenuItem>
      <NavigationMenuItem>
        <NavigationMenuLink href="/docs/components/button" :class="navigationMenuTriggerStyle()">
          Components
        </NavigationMenuLink>
      </NavigationMenuItem>
    </NavigationMenuList>
  </NavigationMenu>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import {
  NavigationMenu,
  NavigationMenuContent,
  NavigationMenuItem,
  NavigationMenuLink,
  NavigationMenuList,
  NavigationMenuTrigger,
  navigationMenuTriggerStyle,
} from '@/Components/NavigationMenu'
</script>
```

## Links and active state

The local `NavigationMenuLink` renders an Inertia `Link`. Pass `href` directly and set `:active="true"` for the current destination. Active styling is not inferred from the URL by this component.

```vue
<NavigationMenuLink
  href="/docs/components/button"
  :active="$page.url.startsWith('/docs/components')"
  :class="navigationMenuTriggerStyle()"
>
  Components
</NavigationMenuLink>
```

A trigger is not a substitute for a link. If its label names a page that users must be able to visit, include that destination as a link inside the panel or make it a direct navigation item.

## Content and viewport

Use `NavigationMenuContent` for a curated set of related destinations with brief descriptions. Avoid placing forms, destructive actions, or multi-step workflows in the panel. The root renders a shared viewport by default; set `:viewport="false"` only when each content panel must position itself independently.

Keep panel widths predictable so opening adjacent items does not cause large layout shifts. The indicator and viewport are optional presentation primitives; links and triggers must remain understandable without them.

## State

The root forwards the current open item through `v-model`. Control it only when another part of the interface must coordinate which panel is open. Do not force a panel open to represent the current page; use the link's active state for that purpose.

## Accessibility and composition

Tab enters and leaves the navigation, arrow keys move among items, and Enter or Space opens a trigger. Escape closes a panel and returns focus. Keep labels unique and order items consistently with the visual layout.

When applying `navigationMenuTriggerStyle()` to a direct link, keep it a real link rather than nesting it in a button. Read the [composition fundamentals](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/fundamentals/composition) before substituting custom trigger children. Use Dropdown Menu or Menubar for commands; this component is for navigation.

Hosted documentation: [Navigation Menu](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/navigation-menu)
